detailed_analysis: _mcp_server_factory.py is a small, focused Layer-3 wiring module (95 lines, one public function, three closures). It builds a per-tool mcp_server CustomizationTypeIO cell, threading the configured secret_policy into the core parse/render functions. Structure and security are sound: the function is well under the 40-line guideline (build_mcp_server_io body is ~80 lines of mostly declarative call construction, but it is split into trivially-readable closures, none exceeding 18 lines), no hardcoded secrets, no injection vectors, and the secret_policy is read from config (not hardcoded) and forwarded to the policy engine. The only real findings are minor maintainability/clean-code points: a duplicated 'secrets_refused' default literal across two branches of one expression, an untyped dialect parameter (Any), and a function-local import of the mcp_server_io callables. Spec conformance is strong: the factory matches the US-10 / integration-protocol port contract, threads secret_policy into both egress boundaries (parse and render) satisfying NFR-15 / Goal 6, and references no concrete tool name internally (the name arrives as a parameter), upholding Goal 5 and the Dependency Rule. No CRITICAL or MAJOR findings.

findings.problem: The default policy value "secrets_refused" is hardcoded as a bare string literal in two distinct branches (line 39 and line 43). The canonical default name is defined elsewhere in the codebase (the protocol and mcp_secret_policy treat secrets_refused as THE default); duplicating the literal here risks drift if the default ever changes and gives the value no named-constant intent marker. Per project guideline No-Hardcoding, this should reference a shared named constant (e.g. DEFAULT_SECRET_POLICY).

The public parameter `dialect` is typed `Any | None`, which discards type checking on a value that is in fact a concrete McpServerDialect (see DEFAULT_MCP_SERVER_DIALECT and the McpServerDialect(...) literals at every call site, e.g. claude.py:115). Using the real type (McpServerDialect | None) on a public boundary parameter would restore mypy coverage and document the contract, per the project's 'type annotations on all public interfaces' rule (NFR-14). The Any is likely a deliberate import-cycle avoidance, but a TYPE_CHECKING import would preserve the annotation without a runtime dependency.

The mcp_server_io callables are imported inside the function body rather than at module top level. This is a recognised lazy-import pattern (likely to break an import cycle between agentic_tool_spec wiring and mcp_server_io), and is benign here, but it hides a real module dependency from static import-graph tooling and readers. If no cycle exists, the import belongs at module scope; if a cycle does exist, a one-line comment naming the cycle would document the intent (POLA). Flagged as INFO only.

notes: NFR-15 / Goal 6: secret_policy is read from config and threaded into BOTH egress boundaries reached by this factory (parse_mcp_server_json and render_mcp_server_json), each of which calls apply_mcp_secret_policy in mcp_server_io. extract_pair_id correctly does NOT apply the policy (it recovers identity only, propagates no content), consistent with the protocol's egress-boundary definition. The policy decision lives in the core mcp_secret_policy module, not the adapter, satisfying 'evaluated by the sync core, not per adapter'.

Goal 5 / Dependency Rule (architecture sec 3, sec 5.1): the factory references NO concrete tool name; agentic_tool_name arrives as a parameter (claude/codex/copilot/cursor/gemini_cli/opencode pass it in). It builds the documented CustomizationTypeIO triple + SharedKeyedMapLayout for mcp_server, matching protocol sec mcp_server and US-10 AC-1/AC-4.

US-10 AC-1 / integration protocol: SharedKeyedMapLayout is constructed with shared_path_config_key, map_key_path, key_field='name', matching the protocol's SharedKeyedMapLayout(shared_path, map_key_path, key_field) contract.

Deprecated-key tolerance (protocol sec mcp_server secret_policy): the factory accepts both secret_policy and the deprecated mcp_server_secret_policy key, matching the documented one-release compatibility window.

verdict_summary: Clean, focused Layer-3 wiring module; conforms to US-10/NFR-15/Goal 5/6; only three INFO-level maintainability nits (duplicated default literal, Any-typed dialect, function-local import).
